evals.prompt: set up sfdt in this salesforce project and wire up a PR pipeline that only deploys what changed, validating against the uat org
evals.expected_output: sfdt init, then sfdt ci init --provider github --type deploy (smart delta deploy with --dry-run validation); explains the .sfdt/ config it created

evals.prompt: I want a scheduled weekly org health report for prod posted to our slack channel. we already have sfdt configured
evals.expected_output: Configures notifications channels (webhookUrlEnv env-var name, not inline secret), uses sfdt monitor all --notify / sfdt ci init --type monitor for the schedule

evals.prompt: sfdt should be able to show me which flows would collide on the same record before I deploy — does it do that? if not, add it
evals.expected_output: Points to the existing sfdt flow conflicts command rather than reimplementing; if extending, follows references/development.md (thin command, lib logic, config template + schema in lockstep)
